# Keeper NL wiring: the live agent boundary replaces the deterministic fallback behind the ONE injected seam

## Context

T2.11 of the harness pillar (#13349). The create spine deliberately left ONE seam open: `routeCreationRequest({request, generate})` takes the agent boundary as a parameter, and the wedge surface falls back to a deterministic blueprint generator. This leaf makes `generate` live: a natural-language creation request reaches a real model call and returns a candidate blueprint — with the route's fail-closed validation unchanged as the safety net (the emit-side of the recorded safety contract; nothing executable can transit regardless of what the model returns).

## The Fix

- A provider-backed `generate` implementation in the create module (`view/create/util/` per convention): prompt = the request + the registered schema vocabulary (from the blueprint schema registry — never a hand-written duplicate of the allowlists); response parsed to a candidate blueprint object; EVERYTHING else stays the route's job (validation, refusal shaping, staging).
- Config-driven model/provider selection (the AiConfig SSOT — read ADR-0019 before authoring; the §3 catalog applies).
- The wedge controller's injectable seam consumes it when configured, keeping the deterministic fallback for unit tests and offline mode (the fallback is a FEATURE — never delete it).
- Prompt-injection posture: the model's output is a CANDIDATE, the shared validator is the gate; a response that isn't a valid blueprint shape lands the standard staged refusal. No streaming in v1.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Typed intent → live model call → candidate blueprint → the SAME `validateBlueprint` gate → accept path (no second validation vocabulary anywhere).
- [ ] Schema vocabulary in the prompt derives from `BLUEPRINT_SCHEMAS` (one source; adding a widget type updates the prompt for free).
- [ ] Provider/model via AiConfig leaves (ADR-0019-clean; no env reads, no threading).
- [ ] Refusal parity: model-garbage, model-timeout, and validation-refusal all land the bounded `{accepted:false, reason, stage}` shapes; nothing throws into the render path.
- [ ] Unit spec with a doubled provider (happy, garbage, timeout); the live-call proof rides the #14734 e2e.

## Out of Scope

Streaming/token-by-token preview · mutation intents (#T2.12 sibling) · multi-schema prompt tuning.

## Related

Parent #13349 (T2.11) · the route/validator (merged) · the wedge surface (PR #14727) · #14644 (the contract) · ADR-0019 (config discipline) · #14734 (the e2e that proves it live).

Claimable — non-Fable implementable; the seam and contract are fully specified.

[ARCH_ALIGNMENT] Intake verdict: **valid model gap, not code-ready yet**. I applied `not-code-ready` + `needs-design` so this does not keep surfacing as claimable implementation work before the consumed contract is pinned.

V-B-A evidence checked before this classification:
- Live issue body/comments: no Contract Ledger matrix is present; labels were only `enhancement` + `ai`; no assignee; no closing PRs.
- Parent #13349 is an `epic`, and the required @neo-gpt epic-review already exists on the parent.
- Source seam is real: `apps/agentos/view/create/util/requestRoute.mjs` accepts injected `generate`, `CreateSurfaceController.mjs` has `generateBlueprint` plus the deterministic fallback, and `blueprintSchema.mjs` owns `BLUEPRINT_SCHEMAS` / `validateBlueprint`.
- Successor-risk: same-day/pre-stale; open-issue search returned only #14762; PR search returned #14727, which shipped the prior controller/fallback surface, not this live provider generator.
- ADR read-gate: ADR-0019 is active and aligned with the intent, but it requires resolved AiConfig leaves at use sites and forbids env re-derivation, pass-along config plumbing, hidden defaults, and runtime mutation.
- Core idiom read-gate: checked `src/core/Base.mjs`, `src/Neo.mjs`, and `src/state/Provider.mjs` for `Neo.setupClass`, config application, and batched Provider `setData()`/hierarchical ownership semantics because this leaf will cross a controller config seam and state-provider flow.

Why this blocks branch work: the ticket introduces or consumes multiple human/agent/runtime surfaces, but asks the implementer to infer the contract from prose. Per `learn/agentos/process/contract-ledger.md`, that negotiation belongs on the ticket before implementation, not in PR review.

Please update the issue body with a Contract Ledger covering at least these rows:

| Target Surface | Contract detail needed before implementation |
|---|---|
| Live `generate` implementation | owning module path, function signature, output shape, parse behavior, and whether it is pure/util or class-backed |
| AiConfig leaves | exact provider/model/timeout/config key names, defaults, env bindings if any, and ADR-0019 read-site authority |
| Prompt vocabulary builder | how `BLUEPRINT_SCHEMAS` is serialized into the prompt, bounded-size behavior, and no duplicate allowlist rule |
| Model response parser | accepted wire format, invalid JSON/non-object handling, schema mismatch handling, and no second validation vocabulary |
| Timeout/provider failure | timeout source, cancellation/timeout behavior, refusal `reason`/`stage` mapping, and no render-path throw |
| Controller fallback selection | when `CreateSurfaceController.generateBlueprint` uses the live generator vs deterministic fallback, offline/test behavior, and how tests inject doubles |
| Evidence | unit spec scope for happy/garbage/timeout plus the dependency on #14734 for live-call proof |

Once those rows are in the body, this can be re-intaked as a likely positive-ROI implementation leaf. Current classification: `needs-narrowing` / `needs-contract-alignment` via the available repo labels `not-code-ready` + `needs-design`, not `duplicate`, not `superseded`, and not `invalid-or-negative-roi`.
